candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A row tied to cost of revenues, shown separately from the selected parent row 'Cost of revenues/COGS'. The selected COGS row matches 'Goods for Resale' and appears not to include this separate depreciation line, so this should be added separately.

candidates.row_label: Amortizations, depreciations and write-downs of tangible and intangible fixed assets
candidates.why: This looks like a broader operating D&A summary below personnel costs, more aligned with overhead/SG&A than with COGS-specific cost of revenues.
A component under the broader operating D&A section, not specifically labelled as cost of revenues; less suitable than the explicit COGS-linked depreciation row.

variables.notes: The explicit COGS row already selected is separate from this cost-of-revenues depreciation row. Adding this row later avoids understating COGS-related D&A and does not appear to double count.

candidates.row_label: Amortizations, depreciations and write-downs of tangible and intangible fixed assets

It also acts as the summary row for the more granular amortization/depreciation lines beneath it, so it is preferred over components to avoid double counting.
Looks like a component of the broader operating D&A summary line, so should not be chosen if the summary row is used.

candidates.why: Looks like a component of the broader operating D&A summary line, so should not be chosen if the summary row is used.
Looks like a component under the broader operating D&A summary line rather than a separate bucket to add on top of it.
This is clearly tied to cost of revenues, not SG&A/overhead.

final_choice.row_label: Amortizations, depreciations and write-downs of tangible and intangible fixed assets

variables.notes: Selected XSGA_COMPONENTS consists only of 'External costs' and 'Personnel costs', and this D&A line is presented separately at the same operating level, so it should be added separately. The detailed amortization/depreciation rows beneath it appear to be components of this summary; using the summary avoids double counting. No separate R&D row was selected earlier, so there is no XRD overlap issue visible here.
